other_observers: Tony Leukering, Tim Smart; Don't know
specific_location: Gregory Canyon, upstream of parking lot.
description: The bird was a typical Piranga and was strikingly bright red overall with all black wings and tail. The grayish bill was rather conical, but long and somewhat the mandible was somewhat bulged. The eyes and legs were also black.Behaviors: Sang periodically from ponderosa pine and douglas-fir stands. Observed perched in trees and chasing nearby female Western Tanager.Call: Similar to that of an American Robin,but with a hoarser quality.Plumage: alternate
